profile_backing.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-fj-lax-linalg-profile-pass56-20260603 rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- linalg --sample-size 30 --measurement-time 3 --warm-up-time 1

lever.name: portable SIMD f64x4 GEMM lanes
lever.description: Attempted safe std::simd f64x4 updates across contiguous output columns inside the existing i-k-j matmul_2d kernel, with scalar tail handling.

alien_primitive.reason: Prior row-block, register-only, transposed-RHS, and row-parallel attempts failed; pass56 tested a lane-level arithmetic primitive while preserving per-cell k order.

isomorphism_proof.ordering: Output rows and columns remained in row-major order.
isomorphism_proof.per_cell_order: Each SIMD lane represented a distinct output column; for each cell, the candidate still applied l=0..k-1 in ascending order.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: No reductions across lanes, partial-sum merges, reassociation, mixed precision, or unsafe intrinsics were introduced.
isomorphism_proof.errors: No public error path changed in candidate state; slice indexing stayed in the same loop nest.

validation.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-fj-lax-simd-pass56-test-20260603 rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax matmul_2d -- --nocapture
validation.details: 3 tests passed in candidate state, including matmul_2d_simd_lane_and_tail_bit_identical_to_ijk

rebench.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-fj-lax-simd-pass56-after-20260603 rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- linalg/matmul_2d_512x512x512_f64 --sample-size 30 --measurement-time 3 --warm-up-time 1

next_primitive.target: recursive/cache-oblivious GEMM or cache-size KC blocking
next_primitive.reason: Lane-level SIMD and coarse row parallelism both regressed. The next deeper primitive should change cache traversal and recursive locality while preserving per-cell ascending-k order.
